title: How secrets leak through logs and errors
description: Credentials passed through code can be captured by logging, error traces, or build output, exposing them outside the files that hold them.
body: Use when reasoning about exposure paths beyond source files. Do not assume a secret kept out of source is therefore contained. A credential in a variable can be printed by a debug log, embedded in an exception message, or emitted in CI build output, where it persists in log stores readable by many. Inspect logging and error handling around secret values. Validation signals include logs and traces showing redacted placeholders instead of credential values. Risk boundary: a secret in a shared log is exposed and must be rotated, so redact at the logging boundary and never log fetched secret values.
